records.ety: [Cf. D. homp a lump, LG. hump heap, hill, stump, possibly akin to E. heap. Cf. Hunch.]

defs: A protuberance; especially, the protuberance formed by a crooked back.
A fleshy protuberance on the back of an animal, as a camel or whale.
a portion of a switchyard with a slanting track in which freight cars may coast without an engine and be sorted through a series of switches.
To form into a hump; to make hump-shaped; to hunch; -- often with up.
quotes: The cattle were very uncomfortable, standing humped up in the bushes.

defs: To put or carry on the (humped) back; to shoulder; hence, to carry, in general.
quotes: Having collected a sufficient quantity, we humped it out of the bush.

defs: To bend or gather together for strenuous effort, as in running; to do or effect by such effort; to exert; -- usually reflexively or with it; as, you must hump yourself.
quotes: A half dozen other negroes, some limping and all scared, were humping it across a meadow.

defs: to sort freight cars by means of a hump.
to engage in sexual intercourse with.
